about_text: New Guy refers to a character in a comic strip made by artist Mallorie Jessica Udischas; aka sweetbeans99. In the comic; a purple-haired character laughs about a 'millionaire gamer-bro douchebag' who got robbed; most likely a reference to PewDiePie getting robbed in early December 2019. Her co-worker; whose nametag reads 'New Guy;' says; 'Hey; how'd you like it if you were robbed?' The purple-haired character then sarcastically remarks that she and the 'new guy' could be friends but then says 'Hell no.' While the comic drew backlash from people who read the comic as the author laughing at PewDiePie's misfortune; others began to sympathize with the 'new guy' character; creating fan art emphasizing his apparent wholesomeness. However; some criticized the comic for inciting attacks on the original artist; accusing the message of 'empathy;' which many claim the characters symbolizes; is disingenuous.

title: Do the Roar
about_text: Do the Roar refers to a scene from the fourth Shrek movie; Shrek Forever After; in which a blonde dad approaches Shrek with his blonde son; canonically known as Butter Pants; asking Shrek to do his 'famous Shrek roar' prompting the kid to say 'Do the roar' in a nasally and deep voice over and over again. The scene was uploaded to YouTube upon the film's release in 2010; resulting in memetic remixes similar to YouTube Poop. Going into the 2020s; the scene gained notoriety and memes on TikTok as many recreated the scene and did Butter Pants' voice.

title: Ginyu Force Pose Tokusentai
about_text: The Ginyu Force Pose; also known as the Tokusentai; is an iconic fighting pose associated with the eponymous mercenary group featured in the popular anime and manga series Dragon Ball. Due to the flamboyant and campy nature of the group's stance; it has been frequently parodied by the fans of the series.

title: Did You Pray Today TikTok Remix
about_text: Did You Pray Today? is a viral TikTok audio and catchphrase that grew popular in the year 2023. The sound is a bass-boosted remix of a 2016 song by rapper Young Thug named 'Gangster Shit.' The sound was popularized on TikTok as a meme towards the start of May 2023 with TikTokers using the sound to lip-sync the lyrics 'Did you pray today? Did you pray today? I could dick you down on some gangster shit. I could put you down on some gangster shit.'

title: Doom Series
about_text: The Doom Series (Japanese: 人類滅亡シリーズ; Jinrui Metsubou Series) refer to a style of Japanese MAD videos featuring a CGI animation of an impact event on planet Earth.

title: Ellen Degeneres Crying
about_text: Ellen Degeneres Crying is an image macro series featuring a screenshot from a video by morning talkshow host Ellen Degeneres about the killing of Breonna Taylor. In summer 2020; many meme'd a screenshot from the video and added the caption; 'When you get so high; you start getting scared.'

title: Wolves Biting Lamb  Agnus

about_text: Wolves Biting Lamb refers to a reaction image used in caption memes that features an oil painting of a serene lamb being bitten by several wolves. The picture; sourced from an upload to ArtStation; began being circulated on Twitter in February 2023.

title: Hitler Did Nothing Wrong
about_text: 'Hitler Did Nothing Wrong' is a statement used for trolling purposes to deny that the acts Adolf Hitler was responsible for during his reign were morally wrong. Over time the phrase become widely used as a snowclone 'X did nothing wrong'.

title: Luigis Death Stare
about_text: Luigi's Death Stare; not to be confused with Weegee; refers to the odd facial expressions worn by the character of Luigi during gameplay in the eighth installment of Nintendo's Mario Kart franchise. Upon the release of the game in late May 2014; the character's creepy gaze quickly became the butt of many jokes and parodies among the fans of Mario Kart.

title: Meridias Beacon  A New Hand Touches The Beacon
about_text: 'A New Hand Touches The Beacon' is a catchphrase referencing Meridia's Beacon; a quest item found in Skyrim that starts a quest many players find tedious. Both the beacon and the catchphrase became a source of memes; particularly YouTube parodies; in which the subject dreads picking up the item.

title: Magneto Was Right
about_text: 'Magneto Was Right' is a slogan used to describe the views of Marvel Comics character and X-Men villain; Magneto; namely his extremist position that war is the only way to achieve equality for the fictional mutant race; as opposed to the X-men's pacifist approach. The phrase has become popular as a response in an X-Men-themed discussion; similar to Peace Was Never An Option.

title: AntiZombie Fortress
about_text: Anti-Zombie Fortress is the nickname given to an abandoned coal mine in Fukuoka Prefecture; Japan; mainly due to its unique structural design that can be seen as highly impervious to zombie raids. While the mine shaft has become a popular destination in Japan for haiyakos (廃虚) or 'urban excursion of abandoned buildings' since the mid-2000s; photographs of the tower became a subject of 'anti-zombie' parodies and online discussions via social news hubsite Reddit in early April 2011.

title: Sapiosexual
about_text: Sapiosexual is sexual identity in which someone is attracted to intelligence or someone's mind rather than their physical appearance. The term was coined in the 1990s but came to be used in mainstream media following OKCupid's change in identity options on user profiles. The term has often been criticized as pretentious and ableist.

title: Recorded with a Potato
about_text: 'Recorded With A Potato' is a phrase commonly found in YouTube comments criticizing the resolution of a video that appears heavily pixelated; blurry or otherwise corrupt. The phrase can be also used as a snowclone in which potato is replaced with another object that is ill-equipped for video recording; such as a toaster; calculator or microwave.

title: Hello I’m Emu Otori Emu Is Meaning Smile
about_text: Hello! I’m Emu Otori! Emu Is Meaning Smile! is a viral audio clip of Hatsune Miku: Colorful Stage character Emu Otori introducing herself and explaining that “Emu” means “smile.” The audio clip first became the subject of memes and video edits on YouTube and TikTok around April 2022; becoming popularized on TikTok in July 2023; often in videos showing Emu Otori in dangerous locations or situations as the audio plays.

title: Lauren Phillips Lifting Alice Merchesi
cues_matched: used to/as/when/in; refer(s|ring)? to; reaction (image/gif/video) or 'a reaction'
about_text: Lauren Phillips Lifting Alice Merchesi; also known as Tall Woman Lifting Short Woman; refers to a still image of pornographic film actress Lauren Phillips lifting her scene partner Alice Merchesi and pushing her against the wall. Starting in March 2017; the image has been used as a reaction and an object labeling meme; later also gaining popularity as source material for redraws.

title: Stop Motion
about_text: Stop Motion is a photography technique in which individually photographed frames are pieced together to create the illusion of movement. While the effect has its roots in the early days of cinema; the craft of stop motion photography has since been adopted by visual artists and hobbyists for a wide range of purposes.

title: Squidward Trying to Sleep
about_text: Squidward Trying to Sleep refers to an image of Squidward Tentacles from a 2005 episode of the animated series SpongeBob SquarePants lying in bed awake and sad or worried; unable to sleep (similar to the Wojak Trying to Sleep meme). A screenshot of the scene became popular in image macros as early as 2013; spreading significantly over the following years; particularly on Tumblr and Reddit. One of the most popular versions of the meme is a photoshop showing Spoons Rattling SpongeBob in the window.

title: Mr Sandman Cat
about_text: Mr. Sandman Cat is a viral video using a nine panel TikTok effect in which the sounds and and lyrics of The Chordette's song 'Mr. Sandman' is sequentially mimicked in each panel by video footage of a cat. In August 2019; the video was shared on Twitter which sparked similar renditions of the video using cats; though in January of 2019; variations of Mr. Sandman nine panel TikTok videos were insularly popular.

title: White Egyptian  American Textbook
about_text: The White Egyptian or White Pharaoh; also known as the 'American Textbook' meme; refers to an unnamed Egyptian Pharaoh character from the slot machine game Pharaoh's Fire; whose design displays distinct European features. Originally believed to be from an American textbook; the White Egyptian appeared often in Nordicist and Eurocentric memes; of both serious and ironic nature.

title: Chopper Crying
about_text: Chopper Crying is a viral image of the character Chopper from One Piece crying in the snow with visible breath coming from his mouth. The screenshot comes from the ninth One Piece movie; Episode of Chopper Plus: Bloom in Winter; Miracle Sakura. It became popularized as a reaction image and a source of redraws and ironic memes in 2022 in connection with the virality of the One Piece Is Real and One Piece Cock Edit memes.

title: When Your Teacher Thinks Youre Studying
about_text: When Your Teacher Thinks You're Studying is an image macro series featuring photographs of food hidden behind school supplies in order to be covertly consumed during class. The pictures are accompanied by variations of the phrasal template 'When your teacher thinks you're studying but you're really eating X.'

title: Manbabies
about_text: Manbabies are simple image macros/photoshoops -- one swaps the head of the man and the baby; like so: This swap/replace idiom is similar to what is seen with memes like Weegee and Little Fatty; as well as other 'just add water' image macros like Gaijin 4Koma and Tourist Guy

title: Sometimes My Genius Its Almost Frightening
about_text: 'Sometimes My Genius…It's Almost Frightening' is a memorable quote uttered by television host Jeremy Clarkson on an episode of the British television series Top Gear.

title: Got Milk
about_text: Got Milk? is an advertisement campaign designed to help increase milk sales in the United States. Since the campaign’s launch in 1993; numerous parodies and imitations have been found both online and in the real world using the snowclone Got X?

title: Nobody
about_text: 'Nobody:'; also known as 'No One:' Tweets and 'Nobody:' Tweets and 'Literally No One:'; is a phrasal template used to mock people who strive to attract attention and tend to provide unsolicited opinions; as well as for observational humor. The format rose to popularity in Autumn 2018 with tweets usually starting with the phrases 'No one:'; 'Nobody:' and 'Me:'; continued with text or visual descriptions of an unprovoked action by a certain individual.

title: Pols Aa Gyi Pols
about_text: Pols Aa Gyi Pols is a viral video and catchphrase popular in India and Indian memes. The phrase originates from a video featuring several men speaking in Punjabi before they are interrupted by flashing blue and red lights from afar; leading someone to exclaim 'oye pols aa gyi pols!' meaning 'oy; the police is coming!' The lights are then shown to have been a false alarm; coming from the headlights of an older bearded man on a bike. The video was first posted to Instagram on an unknown date before it was reposted to YouTube in February 2023 and subsequently went viral as a catchphrase on Instagram in March 2023.

title: KYMtan
about_text: KYM-tan is the name given to a character who was the unofficial humanization of KnowYourMeme.com until she became recognised and was used as the face of the spoiler tags.

title: Communist Bugs Bunny
cues_matched: reaction (image/gif/video) or 'a reaction'; typically; in which
about_text: Communist Bugs Bunny; sometimes known as We Have Bugs Bunny; is a reaction image featuring Loony Toons character Bugs Bunny with a red filter and the hammer and sickle from the Soviet Union flag. Typically; the image is used alongside a caption in which someone claims ownership of something that another subject (depicted as Bugs) claims as “ours.” The meme became popularized in June 2020 across a variety of social media platforms.

title: Bro WTF My Pilot Doing
about_text: Bro WTF My Pilot Doing refers to a photo taken while boarding a plane of the pilot sitting at his seat in the cockpit looking at the screen of what appears to be a laptop. The image on the laptop screen is photoshopped to change the context.

title: Provocateur
about_text: Provocateur refers to a two-panel comic in which the subject instigates a fight between two groups of people. After sending them into violence; the instigator rubs his hands and smiles; evoking the antisemitic character the Happy Merchant. Many have used the meme to express racist; xenophobic and/or bigoted opinions.

title: GO GO MANIAC
about_text: 'GO! GO! MANIAC' is the 1st opening theme song for the 2nd season of a Japanese TV anime K-ON! which was produced by Kyoto Animation. This monumental work in the anime song history has earned many tributes and parodies on YouTube and the Japanese video sharing service Nico Nico Douga (NND).

title: Chipotle Camera Rule  Filming Hack
about_text: The Chipotle Camera Rule; also known as the Chipotle Phone Method; Filming at Chipotle Hack or Recording at Chipotle Hack; refers to a phenomenon of people bringing large and expensive camera equipment to Chipotle locations and recording the employees assembling their burrito or bowl; thereby; pretending like they're filming a video for social media to pressure the employee into giving bigger portions. Others have also simply used their phone to record. The 'method' relied on an alleged 'Chipotle Camera Rule' issued to employees that required them to give larger portions to people recording their orders. It was popularized on TikTok in 2024; then trended on Twitter / X and elsewhere online. As more people tried the method; influencers brought increasingly elaborate camera equipment to Chipotle to parody the concept. Memes about bringing cameras and filming at Chipotle trended en masse in June 2024.

title: Password on the Back of the Router
about_text: Password on the Back of the Router refers to parody images imitating the indecipherable passwords that are typically listed on the back of Wi-Fi routers. In July 2019; image macros containing the jokes widely circulated within the /r/DankMemes community on Reddit.

title: Mind Size  Brain Size

about_text: Mind Size; also known as Brain Size; is a reaction image depicting animated character Megamind (played by Will Ferrell) grinning next to a washing machine dial (which uses the same image as the Oof Size Large meme) labeled 'Mind size' that is set to “Mega.” A second version depicting YouTuber Markiplier with his forehead humorously enlarged uses the meme Yeah; This is Big Brain Time with the reaction image showing his face next to the dial labeled “Brain size” that is set to 'Large.' Each of these variants are typically used as reaction images to show the intellect or wittiness of the above caption or image in the top panel. Though usually genuine; they can also be used sarcastically.

title: I Can Count to Potato
about_text: 'I Can Count to Potato' is a controversial catchphrase that is meant to indicate a lack of intelligence and usually paired with photographs of people who appear to have been diagnosed with learning disabilities. The phrase is sometimes used in image boards and forums in reacting to comments that are deemed unintelligent. Due to the insensitive nature of the meme; it has been criticized for perpetuating ableism[7]; a type of discrimination against people with disabilities.

title: Aunt Cass Checks Your Browser History
about_text: Aunt Cass Checks Your Browser History refers to an NSFW POV animation by Newgrounds user Redmoa where Aunt Cass from Big Hero 6 talks about checking the viewer's browser history and finding they're interested in a 'specific type' of adult content; then offering to let the viewer experience the 'real thing' before disrobing and engaging in a sexual act with a faceless character; meant to be the viewer. The video was uploaded to Newgrounds in September 2021 and inspired numerous safe-for-work (SFW) edits altering various aspects of the animation to change the context. The meme is similar to the Busty Aunt Cass meme that went viral at the end of 2020.

title: Pineapple Dad
about_text: Pineapple Dad refers to a series of photoshops inspired by Reddit post that features a father sitting on a stool and holding a pineapple.

title: ASCII Normal Heart Rate
about_text: ASCII Normal Heart Rate is a comparative phrasal template that expresses one's excitement caused by the meme's subject through a series of text characters (ASCII art) that are comprised to resemble a medical cardiac rate monitor

title: Eggman Pisses on the Moon
about_text: Eggman Pisses On the Moon; also known as Shadow the Hedgehog Is a Bitch-Ass Motherfucker; refers to an audio clip taken from YouTube channel SnapCube in which a man improvises dialogue over a cutscene from Sonic Adventure 2; acting as the villainous Dr. Robotnik; aka Eggman; saying he was urinating on the moon. The audio has been remixed and placed over other footage.

title: Get Along Shirt
about_text: The 'Get Along' Shirt is an oversized t-shirt that is meant to be worn by bickering children as a disciplinary method. The humorous concept gained much of its momentum after a photograph of two children strapped together by a t-shirt reading 'The 'We Will Get Along' Shirt' was posted to Reddit in November 2012.

title: Get That Thing Out Of My Face
about_text: Get That Thing Out of My Face; also known as Cracker Meme; is an exploitable webcomic involving a bird screaming at a cracker before biting it and enjoying the taste. The comic became a popular object labeling meme in October 2019.

title: Emo Kylo Ren
about_text: Emo Kylo Ren is a parodic anonymous Twitter account where the supposed user is the character Kylo Ren from the film Star Wars Episode VII: The Force Awakens; except with the inflection of an emo teen. Following the popularity of the film; the Twitter account became very popular.

title: Follow Me To
about_text: Follow Me To or #FollowMeTo refers to a series of photographs shot from the first person perspective of the photographer's subject pulling them by the hand in different locations. The photographs; made popular on Instagram by photographer Murad Osmann and his girlfriend Natalia Zakharova; have been mimicked by numerous other users and have been the subject of parody; image macros and photoshops.

title: Welcome to the Rice Fields Motherfucker
about_text: Welcome to the Rice Fields; Motherfucker is a quote by Filthy Frank from his video 'HOW TO SAY HELLO IN 30 LANGUAGES.' It became a popular quote and catchphrase among his fans and has spread in various image macros.

title: Running Joker
about_text: Running Joker refers to several exploitable images of 2019 film Joker titular character Joker running; based on photographs made during the filming of one of the scenes of the movie. The exploitables have seen moderate use online; with the meme seeing a surge in popularity following the premiere of the film in October 2019.

title: Selena Gomez Crying
about_text: Selena Gomez Crying is a reaction image and photoshop meme based on a still shot of American teen pop idol on the brink of crying during a radio interview in December 2013. The meme is not to be confused with the Selena Gomez Crying About Deportations viral video and meme from January 2025.

title: Shut Seagull

about_text: Shut Seagull refers to a photoshopped screenshot from the Inhaling Seagull video of a seagull with its neck extended and its mouth being held closed by the 'OK Hand Emoji' (👌). The word 'shut' is written at the bottom of the image. It is often used in image macros and as a reaction image.

title: Im So Alone
about_text: I'm So Alone is an exploitable comic series in which characters proclaim their love for a specific hobby or fandom; though immediately followed by the realization that they have become social outcasts as a result. While the comics were initially popularized through the My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic fandom; many other fictional and original characters have been since incorporated into the comics.

title: Me When I Post X On X Put My Phone On Vibrate And Put It In My Ass
about_text: Me When I Post X On X; Put My Phone On Vibrate; And Put It In My Ass is a recaption meme and phrasal template most often accompanied with an image macro of a crying anime girl in bed to represent the idea of posting; writing or tweeting a subjectively controversial opinion into a server; social-media circle or forum that would prompt said community to retaliate en masse; therefore prompting one's notifications to vibrate frequently and prompt an orgasm or pleasure in the 'ass' of the meme creator. The caption was used with other image macros and GIFs as well; spreading across platforms like Reddit; Twitter and Instagram from mid-to-late 2022. The NSFW concept; however; dates back to 2014 Tumblr.

title: 911 Tourist Guy
about_text: 9/11 Tourist Guy (also known as 'WTC Guy' or 'Tourist of Death') is an exploitable photoshop meme and an internet hoax based on a photograph of a man standing on the observation deck of a building overlooking Manhattan with an airplane flying towards the vantage point. The image; which was manipulated to appear as if it was taken during the World Trade Center attack of September 11th; 2001; spread across a wide range of discussion forums and online news sites shortly after the tragic events and ignited a series of photoshopped derivatives as well as conspiracy theories surrounding the man's identity.

title: Sigma Stare
about_text: Sigma Stare is a slang expression used to describe a type of unsmiling; direct stare associated with Sigma Males; usually ironically. Starting in May 2021; the phrase has been used as a humorous caption for images of characters staring; and later gained an association with 'After Party' by Don Toliver in video edits.

title: Dancing Stormtrooper
about_text: Dancing Stormtrooper (a.k.a StormHumper) is a short video clip starring a Star Wars fan dressed as Imperial Stormtrooper and dancing to an acapella rendition of Johann Strauss' waltz composition The Blue Danube. Since its debut on YouTube in March 2007; the clip has been viewed over 26 million times and generated over 100 response videos.

title: Diane Foxington
about_text: Diane Foxington is an anthropomorphic fox character from the Dreamworks animated film The Bad Guys; released in April 2022. She is an ex-thief and the Governor of California and the love interest for Mr. Wolf. The character inspired fan art; including rule 34; following its release.

title: Unimpressed Lizard
about_text: Unimpressed Lizard is a photoshop meme based on an image of a lizard gazing at the camera with a grumpy expression on its face. The cutout image of the lizard is superimposed onto a variety of base images; in a similar vein to many other photoshop memes and image macros that have been inspired by photographs of bored looking subjects.

title: Boss Makes a Dollar I Make a Dime
about_text: Boss Makes a Dollar; I Make a Dime is the start of a rhyming couplet online which usually ends with 'That's why I poop on company time.' The couplet has also been used as a snowclone where 'poop' is replaced with a different activity a worker might use to shirk their duties while on the job.

title: In Ur Base
about_text: 'I'm in your base killing your dudes'; often written as 'im in ur base killin ur d00dz'; is a popular gaming catchphrase used to indicate one's pwnage over another player; as in 'Guess what? I'm humiliating you and you don't even know it.'

title: Gwenchana  Gwenchanayo
about_text: Gwenchana or Gwenchanayo (괜찮아; 괜찮아요 in Korean) is a prevalent Korean slang expression that translates to 'Are you okay?' or 'It’s fine!' depending on intonation. The expression is often used in K-Drama series and became a viral sound on TikTok in early September 2023; with users posting ironic memes; comedy skits and funny videos based on the Korean catchphrase.

title: No Fun Allowed
about_text: No Fun Allowed is an expression which is often used in Internet forums to mock those who insist others stay on topic in discussion threads.

title: Big Mike
about_text: Big Mike is a nickname for American lawyer; former First Lady and writer Michelle Obama (wife of former President Barack Obama) that references the conspiracy theory of Michelle Obama secretly being a man. While it's unclear who initially created the nickname; it has been heavily used since as early as October 2020 by Republicans and critics of the former First Lady in various memes; jokes and posts on social media platforms like X / Twitter; Reddit and Facebook; among others. Many of the posts using the nickname are often criticized as having transphobic and racist connotations; which typically claim Michelle Obama is a transgender woman by showing old photos of her alongside Barack Obama or editing images of her to add masculine features.

title: MeToo
about_text: #MeToo is a hashtag campaign that circulated on Twitter and other social media platforms in which actress Alyssa Milano encouraged survivors of sexual assault and harassment to post '#MeToo' or 'Me Too' to raise awareness and highlight its commonality.

title: Who Wants Change Who Wants to Change

about_text: Who Wants Change? Who Wants to Change? refers to a series of memes based on a 2012 webcomic by Brazilian artist Lute. In memes; the comic is typically used as an exploitable image macro to parody people's unwillingness to make sacrifices necessary to achieve their desired goal by changing the text in the original cartoon's word bubbles.

title: Gurting  Gurt Slang
about_text: Gurting or Gurt is an ironic slang term and spinoff of the 'Gurt: Yo' meme that means doing something that's very smart but also very dangerous; which is a reference to the 'What They're Doing Is Very Smart but Also Very Dangerous' meme. The term was popularized as the subject of memes in mid-April 2025 and is often used alongside other niche slang terms like 'IKIAB.'

title: Sydney Sweeneys AntiWoke Boobs
about_text: Sydney Sweeney's Anti-Woke Boobs refers to a sentiment among some conservatives online that actress Sydney Sweeney's March 2024 appearance on Saturday Night Live; in which she notably wore outfits that emphasized her cleavage; was somehow an affront to woke culture. This sentiment led to an editorial in Canada's National Post; leading to jokes and debate online.

title: Bre N Mia Laughing

about_text: Bre N Mia Laughing; also known as Bre and Mia Laughing; refers to a TikTok video in which two girls act out listening to someone; presumably at a party; and bursting into laughter. The clip has been widely duetted with people telling stories or facts about themselves which might cause the girls to break out laughing. These are usually self-deprecating and make it seem as though the girls are laughing at the duetter. It is used similarly to reaction image memes like Girls Laughing.

title: Sleeping Squidward
cues_matched: used to/as/when/in; refer(s|ring)? to; reaction (image/gif/video) or 'a reaction'
about_text: Sleeping Squidward; also known as Squidward's Open Eyes; refers to a photoset featuring two images of the SpongeBob SquarePants character Squidward opening and closing his eyes. Online; it is frequently used as a reaction image to convey feelings of shock upon remembering an assigned task that has not been completed.

title: RTX Off  RTX On
about_text: RTX On / RTX Off is a resolution comparison meme based on Nvidia's recently unveiled RTX line of graphics processors; which use 'ray-tracing' technology to more accurately depict how a light source would interact with objects in a virtual space. The technology was touted as perhaps the next evolution in graphics.

title: Leonardo DiCaprio Laughing
about_text: Leonardo DiCaprio Laughing is an image macro of Calvin Candie played by Leonardo DiCaprio in the 2012 film Django Unchained which features the character smiling while holding a drink. The image gained Photoshop edits starting in November 2017 and became a popular reaction image in August 2020 on Reddit.

title: Nightmares Fear Factory
about_text: Nightmares Fear Factory[1] is a haunted house attraction located in Niagara Falls; Canada. Since the early 2000s; they have photographed the reactions of their visitors as they go through the venue to capture their candid fear. In the past few years; collections of these photos have gone viral every October.

title: Gru Hella Gay
about_text: Gru Hella Gay is an image macro series featuring the protagonist Gru from the Despicable Me franchise; which often contains captions indicating that the other internet users are 'gay' based on the relative position of their comments; chat messages and forum posts.

title: DumpStarWars
about_text: #DumpStarWars is a hashtag spread on Twitter that calls for a boycott of the upcoming film Star Wars: Rogue One after a rumor spread that scenes in the film had been altered to promote an anti-Trump agenda.

title: What Are You Gonna Do Stab Me
about_text: What Are You Gonna Do; Stab Me? or Quote From Man Stabbed refers to a screenshot of a newscast by station KHQ about a man who got stabbed; showing a quote from the victim spoken right before the stabbing; saying; 'what are you gonna do; stab me?' The image first started circulating online in mid-2012 and is paired with reaction images that highlight the quote's absurdity. The image's exact origin was unknown until a 2020 BNet story was published where the author reached out to a KHQ reporter; who offered a transcript of the report. The video of the news report has not been recovered.

title: Behold My Stuff
about_text: 'Behold! My Stuff' is a memorable quote uttered by the character Skurge in the 2017 Marvel superhero film Thor: Ragnarok. Many have used the scene in a series of image macros and remix videos.

title: He Could Be Any One of Us
about_text: 'He Could Be Any One of Us;' sometimes misquoted as 'It Could Be Any One of Us'; is a memorable quote said by character Spy in the 'Meet the Spy' Team Fortress 2 animated short. Starting in October 2018; a still image of Spy paired with the quote gained popularity as an image macro.

title: Youre Not My Dad
about_text: You're Not My Dad!; also known by the hashtag #NOODLEHEAD is a phrase uttered by a young child in a viral video on Vine uploaded by user Jessi Lockett. The child yells at the camera; saying 'You're Not My Dad! You always wanna hear something!'; then calls the person holding the camera an 'ugly a** f***in' noodlehead.' Both the initial and last lines of the video have been used in mashup videos on Vine; or videos where the soundbyte is paired with other clips for comedic effect; similar to The 'Lebron James' Kid.

title: Oppression Olympics
about_text: 'Oppression Olympics' refers to arguments in which inequalities faced by a group are dismissed for being considered less important than those faced by another group. While it was originally used inside feminist circles to address race-related grievances within the feminist movement; the term has been used online to mock those who seek approval or praise for being more disadvantaged than others.

title: SpongeBob I Fucking Love X
about_text: SpongeBob 'I Fucking Love X' refers to a recaption and exploitable format that re-edits an Impact font meme showing SpongeBob SquarePants screaming; 'I Fucking Love War I Want To Fucking Kill People For Money And Destroy Cultures And History.' Typically; meme creators replace the word 'War' and other words in the bottom caption to reference various activities; events or nouns; in general; that they 'fucking love;' stating the reason for their devotion. Although the meme is believed to have been shared on iFunny first; the earliest known repost comes from late 2021 on Instagram. Additionally; the image of SpongeBob screaming has been used in precursor meme formats like He Don't Miss; and the phrasal template is similar to both I Fucking Love Cocaine and I Love Lean!!!

title: Its Just An Ordinary Krabby OH MY GOODNESS
about_text: It's Just An Ordinary Krabby- OH MY GOODNESS is a quote from SpongeBob SquarePants character Plankton; which inspired video remixes and pictures based on the corresponding scene. The scene features the character Nat returning a rotten Krabby Patty sandwich to the character Plankton. Online; people have replace the sandwich with other humorous or revulsing objects.

title: Freddie Mercury Rage Pose
about_text: Freddie Mercury Rage Pose is a rage comic character made from a photo of deceased British musician and former lead vocalist for the rock band Queen Freddie Mercury. The image is typically used to indicate that an extraordinary feat has been accomplished; similar to the Fuck Yea illustration.

title: Brienne of Tarth Writing
about_text: Brienne of Tarth Writing refers to an image macro featuring a shot from the American fantasy series Game of Thrones in which the character Ser Brienne of Tarth writes in the Book of the Kingsguard. Online; people juxtaposed the screenshot with humorous images of what they imagine she is writing (similar to Justin Timberlake's Super Bowl Halftime Show Selfie Kid).

title: What If Zelda Was A Girl
about_text: What If Zelda Was A Girl?; otherwise known as Link and Zelda Confusion; refers to a common mistake by people with little to no prior knowledge of The Legend of Zelda video game series confusing Princess Zelda for the protagonist; Link. The question 'what if Zelda was a girl?' became a copypasta used to troll users on 4chan sometime around 2010; spreading across social media in the years since.

title: Mmmm Myes Pet Froge
about_text: Mmmm Myes Pet Froge refers to a GIF caption of a frog being petted that inspired similar GIF captions in early-to-mid 2020. A series of Twitch emotes using the hand petting the frog known as PETTHE Emotes began circulating in May 2020.

title: Pussy Tight Pussy Clean Pussy Fresh
about_text: Pussy Tight; Pussy Clean; Pussy Fresh refers to a viral sound on TikTok mashing up the instrumental to Melanie Martinez' song 'Soap' with distorted; robotic sounding lyrics from Shenseea's song 'Pon Mi.' The sound was posted to TikTok in March 2022 and went viral in lip dub skits 21st Century Humor style videos over the following months.

title: Bark Souls  Dark Souls IRL Edits
about_text: Bark Souls; Bork Souls and Dark Souls IRL Edits refer to videos of real-life actions resembling boss behavior in video game Dark Souls and its direct and spiritual sequels; particularly of dogs acting in a similar way to Dark Souls boss Great Grey Wolf Sif. The videos are usually edited to include music and UI elements from the game.

title: Kekmaga
about_text: Kekma.net; formerly Kekma.ga; is a shock site displaying NSFL content which has been used in raid attacks on several subreddits and Discord servers. The site contains a rapidly flashing NSFL image accompanied by a high-pitched ear rape of a person screaming; as well as an NSFL video in the center of the page. Upon opening; the page enters fullscreen mode; while an attempt to leave the pages produces a confirmation window; slowing down the exiting process.

title: Do I Look Like I Need Your Power
about_text: Do I Look Like I Need Your Power? refers to a scene from the trailer for Sonic The Hedgehog 2 where Sonic says 'Face it; you're never going to get my power;' then spin dashes at Knuckles only to be stopped mid-air by him; Knuckles says; 'Do I look like I need your power?' and slams Sonic into the ground. The scene inspired memes including image macros; object labeling memes and video edits starting in December 2021.

title: Groomer Wojak
about_text: Groomer Wojak refers to an -Oomer Wojak casting a leary look; with his mouth slightly open in excitement. Originating from the The 35-Year-Old Groomer meme; the Wojak has since gained popularity as a way to depict those who intend to exploit impressionable people; sexually or otherwise.

title: Rage Quit
about_text: Rage Quit; also seen as RageQuit in one word; is Internet slang commonly used to describe the act of suddenly quitting a game or chatroom after either an argument; extreme frustration; or loss of the game.

title: Titanic Lobsters
about_text: Titanic Lobsters refers to a series of memes based on the idea that live lobsters stored in the kitchen of the passenger liner Titanic would be celebrating the sinking of the ship. The trope originated in 2011 with a Twitter joke and gained notable spread in memes on Reddit in May 2019.

title: Crying While Eating
about_text: Crying While Eating is a series of homemade movies depicting people crying while they eat something. Originally produced by a group of friends as a contest submission for the Contagious Media Showdown; a viral media competition hosted by Jonah Peretti in May 2005; the series went onto inspire several dozens of copycat videos on YouTube.

title: Muito Real
cues_matched: used to/as/when/in; refer(s|ring)? to; reaction (image/gif/video) or 'a reaction'
about_text: Muito Real; Portuguese for 'So Real' or 'Very Real;' is a catchphrase used in reaction to the announcement of Mothman appearing in the upcoming video game Shin Megami Tensei V (SMT); a half-man-half-moth creature with no arms and a stunned look on its face. The phrase; popularized by the Twitter account SMT Portugal; took off following the character's announcement in late June 2021 as a way to show exaggerated excitement over it. The phrase has been used as a meme by SMT Portugal and Portuguese SMT fans since as early as 2018. It is sometimes used to refer to Mothman directly as an alternate name; although this was not the original intention.

title: Jubi Slide  Jubislide
about_text: The Jubi Slide also spelled Jubislide is a dance move created by dancer and TikToker @jubi2fye in early 2022 that makes it appear as if the dancer is smoothly sliding across the ground sideways; somewhat resembling a sideways moonwalk. The dance was popularized on TikTok around March 2022 for the 'Aye Bay Bay' dance challenge and was further popularized in late 2023 with a similar dance challenge set to the song 'A Pimp Named Slickback' by LAKIM.

title: fortniteburgernet
about_text: fortniteburger.net is a shock site that refers the user to a Rule 34 image of Wreck-It Ralph titular character Ralph. The site gained online notoriety in September 2019.

title: Buy the Dip
about_text: Buy the Dip; sometimes written Buy the Fucking Dip and abbreviated to BTFD; is a catchphrase and piece of financial advice widely shared among investors at moments when the market is trending downwards. It exhorts participants in the market to double down on an asset that is doing poorly; buying more of it in hopes that the price will recover and they will eventually make a profit. During the early 2010s alongside the rise of cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin; the phrase became a common slang expression among the crypto community whenever various coins dipped in value. It is also used similarly in meme stock circles online.

title: Do You Love The Colour Of The Sky
about_text: 'Do you love the colour of the sky?' : “Do you love the colour of the sky?” refers to a series of long-form images depicting the color gradient of the sky throughout a 24-hour cycle. On Tumblr ; these images have inspired quite a few parodies featuring an irrelevant character or imagery at the bottom. In May 2020; the original meme gained popularity as a part of the Stretched Instagram Posts trend.

title: Phineas Touched Ferb And Thats Why He Quiet
about_text: Phineas Touched Ferb And That's Why He Quiet is a copypasta and series of memes based on a viral Facebook post by Ivy Moenae reading; 'Now they tryna say Phineas touched Ferb and that's why he quiet.' The post; which references the animated series Phineas and Ferb; was made in March 2019 and garnered viral spread throughout the 2020s; inspiring memes referencing and directly using the text of the post.

title: HisNameIsAlex
about_text: #HisNameIsAlex is a hashtag used in response to a viral TikTok video of a mother deadnaming her son Alex and encouraging his followers to do the same. The video spread on Twitter and TikTok in mid-August 2020 and caused his mother to be fired from her job. TikTokers continued to update viewers on Alex's situation despite his deleted social media accounts but there has been no proof of Alex's safety.

title: This Is Sparta
about_text: This is Sparta! is a catchphrase usually used in images that parody the scene from the film 300 where the main protagonist Leonidas; King of Sparta; declines peace with the Persians by shouting at the Persian Messenger 'This is Sparta!' and kicking him into a large well proceeded by the killing of the other Persian messengers. (See also: Falcon Kick).

title: He Wants to Order
about_text: He Wants to Order refers to a series of redraw memes based on a photograph of a man in the passenger seat of a car leaning and crawling over the driver to order food at a drive-thru window; captioned with a Snapchat banner reading; 'He wants to order.' The photo was posted to Tumblr in July 2022 and Twitter in August; inspiring numerous redraws over the following day; including a 'She wants to order' variant.

title: Abby Bite Edits
about_text: Abby Bite Edits or Biteposting refers to a series of memes in which characters from various franchises are edited as if their fingers are being bitten off; with the meme format based on a still image of The Last of Us Part II character Abby biting Ellie's fingers. The format originated on 4chan's /v/ board in June 17th; 2020; gaining a major spread on the board.

title: Baby Name Chalkboard List
about_text: Baby Name Chalkboard List is a viral image macro of a pregnant white woman named McKinli Hatch standing next to a list of uncommon baby names that have been written on a chalkboard. Since the image went viral; the woman in the photograph; McKinli Hatch; has posted numerous variations of her chalkboard list.

title: Delet This
about_text: Delet This; often intentionally misspelled and accompanied by a reference to or a photograph of a gun; is a copypasta used on message boards and comments systems to threaten; often ironically; that the original post is too extreme.

title: I Might Go Homeless
about_text: I Might Go Homeless?; also known as Supporting X in a Y Family; refers to a series of exploitable memes based on an opening panel of a webcomic drawn by a young person supporting Palestine in a family who supports Israel in the 2023-2024 Israel-Hamas Conflict. In June 2024; after the author of the comic announced that she briefly became homeless; their thread was parodied online; with users editing the opening panel and parodying the caption by using it as a phrasal template.

title: TrumpPence Logo
about_text: Trump-Pence Logo refers to the official insignia of Donald Trump's 2016 presidential campaign redesigned and unveiled by his fundraising committee to incorporate the Republican presidential nominee's newly appointed running mate and Indiana's Republican governor; Mike Pence. Upon its debut in July 2016; the image instantly became a subject of online mockeries due to the suggestive nature of its interlocked letters T and P.

title: It Lives In My Sink
about_text: It Lives In My Sink refers to a series of memes where one character tells another 'it lives in my sink' and panics about something living in the sink; depicted in the meme as a photo of an eyeball peering up out of a sink drain. The eyeball photo was first uploaded to Twitter in September 2021 and is actually a glass eye that fell into the drain while its owner was washing it. The images inspired numerous photoshops the month it was published; as well as becoming an exploitable.

title: Swarthy Genderfluid Child

about_text: Swarthy; Gender-fluid Child refers to a description author Juniper Fitzgerald used to describe her child in a tweet; leading to mockery and parodies similar to those which followed the 30-50 Feral Hogs tweet.

title: My Goals Are Beyond Your Understanding
about_text: My Goals Are Beyond Your Understanding is a memorable quote uttered by the character Reverse Flash on the American superhero television series The Flash. Online; a screenshot of the character has been used as a reaction image macro used to describe one's strength and confidence.

title: Keemstar Is a Bald Retard
about_text: Keemstar Is a Bald Retard refers to a viral post made by a hijacker of streamer Ninja's X / Twitter account in February 2020. In late 2023; a screenshot of the post achieved virality online; with the line having been repeatedly used as an insult against influencer; podcaster and streamer KEEMSTAR since.

title: Last Online 5 Years Ago
about_text: Last Online: 5 Years Ago is a catchphrase used to express an abrupt end of communication with an online friend. The phrase mimics the language found on various online message boards and video games that allows users to see when a player or board member was last active.

title: Why Are Chickens So Funny
about_text: Why Are Chickens So Funny? is a viral TikTok video in which a young girl tells a joke that goes; 'Why are chickens so funny?' the punchline being; 'because;' spoken in the cadence of a chicken clucking. The video was uploaded to TikTok in October 2022 and went viral as an original sound over the following months.

title: Skinwalker
about_text: Skinwalker is a shape-shifting mythological creature in Navajo culture. Sometimes referred to as a witch; a skin-walker has the ability to transform into and/or possess an animal. In internet culture; skinwalkers have been a widespread subject of greentexts on 4chan's /x/ and /k/ boards; gaining additional presence in memes in early 2020. In October 2021; memes about skinwalkers achieved major popularization online; primarily on TikTok and iFunny.

title: Star Wars Kid
about_text: Star Wars Kid is the nickname given to Ghyslain Raza; a Canadian teenager who filmed himself fighting against imaginary sentries with a golf-ball retriever; as though it were a double-sided light saber wielded by the antagonist Darth Maul in Star Wars: Episode I. Over the last decade; it is estimated that the original; unmodified Star Wars Kid video has accumulated over one billion views.

title: Hey Andy Sweetie
about_text: 'Hey; Andy; Sweetie is a comic featuring a dialogue between the video game character Princess Peach and a young boy; in which Peach greets the boy by saying 'Hey; Andy; sweetie.' The comic has spawned numerous parodies and remixes on YouTube; Tumblr and DeviantArt.

title: Nigerian Scams
about_text: Nigerian Scams are a common type of advance-fee fraud schemes facilitated by spambots that can send large volumes of e-mails at once. Although its origin is not limited to Nigeria; the most infamous specimen of this e-mail scam is associated with a Nigerian sender posing as a significant political or royal figure and soliciting the reader to wire a specific amount of cash; which would supposedly allow the sender to access their savings account and reward the benefactor with more money than originally borrowed.

title: Ayy LMAO
about_text: 'Ayy LMAO' is an expression and Tumblr hashtag which is almost always associated with pictures of aliens.

title: Name Something You Can Say During Dinner and During Sex
about_text: Name Something You Can Say During Dinner and During Sex refers to a social game in which people give a prompt asking for a double entendre that can apply during a specific scenario and during sex. While early iterations of the game have appeared online for years; the game grew more popular in December of 2019 as people offered more absurd and topical prompts.

title: Child Porn 3 Bowing to the Paul God
cues_matched: used to/as/when/in; refer(s|ring)? to; captioned; in which
about_text: Child Porn 3 Bowing to the Paul God refers to a humorously captioned photograph of basketball player Chris Paul being knocked down by fellow player Paul George during a 2021 NBA match between the Los Angeles Clippers and Phoenix Suns. The caption of the photo; in which Chris Paul is captured mid-fall; references Chris Paul's nickname; CP3; intentionally misinterpreting it as 'Child Porn 3.' Both the photograph and the nickname saw further use online in the following years; with the image being used as an exploitable; often paired with the X Bowing to Y God phrasal template; and the nickname being used to refer to the player.

title: WeaselPecker
about_text: WeaselPecker; a portmanteau of “weasel” and “woodpecker;” is a photoshop meme based on a well-timed photograph of a weasel riding on the back of a flying woodpecker bird. Since entering online circulation through Twitter in early March 2015; the photograph has given way to a series of photoshopped parodies in which other subjects and fictional characters are shown riding alongside or in lieu of the weasel.

title: Millennial Whoop
about_text: The 'Millennial Whoop' is a neologism referring to any sequence of notes alternating between the 5th and 3rd notes on a major scale; which are often heard during vocal segments in a variety of contemporary pop songs.

title: My Heart Is Cold
cues_matched: used to/as/when/in; refer(s|ring)? to; captioned; represent(s|ing)?
about_text: My Heart Is Cold; continued My Moves Are Bold; also known as Putin (My Heart Is Cold); refers to a Vladimir Putin song and animated music video made by the YouTube channel Bad History in 2020. In early 2023; the video and song became an exploitable meme template on TikTok when creators captioned the song's chorus and Putin's verse; 'My Heart Is Cold. My Moves Are Bold.' A greenscreen of Putin sitting in a chair and him dancing like a Gopnik was used as well. The memes were made to represent being unbothered in a given scenario.

title: 5Head
about_text: 5Head is a Twitch Emote showing a picture of Twitch streamer Cadberry of the 4Head emote edited such that his forehead is much larger than the rest of his face. The emote is used to react to moments of great intelligence; or to mock streamer's mistakes; and is particularly popular in the chat of streamer xQc.

title: Me Trying to Live a Normal Productive Life
about_text: Me Trying to Live a Normal Productive Life; also known as Monk Temptation or Lead Us Not Into Temptation; refers to a painting of a monk praying; eyes closed and head to the sky; while a woman in the background tries to tempt him. The image saw use in image macro memes and as an exploitable image labeling format in 2017.

title: I For One Welcome Our New Insect Overlords
about_text: “I; for one; welcome our new insect overlords” is a memorable quote from the 1977 film adaptation of H.G. Wells' short story Empire of the Ants. In online conversations; the phrase can be used to suggest that an outsider group or entity is powerful enough to rule over humanity. In discussion forums and chatrooms; its phrasal template 'I; for one; welcome our new X overlords' has been widely used to express mock submission towards an obsessively controlling individual for the sake of humor.

title: Donald Duck Boner
about_text: Donald Duck Boner refers to a series of still images and gifs from Disney cartoons that feature Donald Duck with large bump in his mid-section. Many online have pointed out that the placement of the bump makes it appear as though the kid-friendly cartoon character has an erection.

title: Matrix Morpheus
about_text: Matrix Morpheus is an advice animal image macro series featuring a screen capture of the fictional character Morpheus[1] (played by Lawrence Fishburne) from The Matrix franchise.[2] The captions typically follow a two line format with the first line reading 'What if I Told You;' followed by a revelation that often belittles futile or petty behavior; in a similar vein to the advice animal characters Conspiracy Keanu and Sudden Clarity Clarence.

title: My Face When MFW  That Face When TFW
about_text: 'My Face When…' (also shortened as '>mfw') is a colloquial phrase commonly used on 4chan imageboards as the opening line of reply posts and greentext stories in particular. The phrase is used in forum discussions to discuss how users would respond to a given situation; whether it be hypothetical or based on experiences of real life. 'Mfw' posts can be also accompanied by a reaction face image.

title: Hurricane Shark
about_text: Hurricane Shark refers to an illegitimate photograph of a shark swimming on a city highway. The photograph has been used during multiple natural disasters and floods; warning people that sharks are swimming through the flood waters.

title: Germany Oneesan  Anime Girls Nazi Past
about_text: Germany Oneesan / Anime Girl's Nazi Past is an object-labeling image by Twitter user naporitan1946 of a smiling anime girl consoling a crying boy with her hand on his head; while a sepia-toned photograph in the foreground shows her posing in front of two hanging bodies wearing what appears to be a Nazi uniform; revealing her dark past.

title: Brace Yourselves Winter is Coming
about_text: Brace Yourselves; Winter is Coming; also known as Imminent Ned; is an image macro meme featuring the character Eddard 'Ned' Stark (played by actor Sean Bean) from the HBO series Game of Thrones; a television adaption of George R. R. Martin's epic fantasy novel series A Song of Ice and Fire. Its phrasal template 'Brace Yourselves; X is Coming' can be versatilely used to forewarn or proclaim the impending arrival of a highly anticipated event; a product or an internet meme.

title: CNNs FactsFirst Ad
about_text: CNN's #FactsFirst Ad refers to a viral advertisement by CNN in which a narrator informs the viewer they are looking at an apple regardless of whether or not someone attempts to convince them it is a banana.

title: Winrar
about_text: Winrar is a .ZIP and .RAR file archiving and compression utility first released in 1995. It is commonly known to people as having an infinite free trial; as well as quick and easy installation and usage.

title: Casey Frey Get TF Out of My Way Video

A month after the video was posted; screenshots of the bully and the wood-cutting character began seeing use on Twitter as reaction images.

title: Well I Just Wanna Say That Im A Huge Fan

title: Waking Up in the Morning Thinking About So Many Things  Gias Sad Song
about_text: Waking Up in the Morning; Thinking About So Many Things; also known as Gia's Sad Song; refers to a 2011 song by Gia Giudice; daughter of The Real Housewives of New Jersey star Teresa Giudice; written about a conflict between the Giudice and Gorga families on the show. In March 2021; the song gained viral popularity on TikTok with users sharing awkward or cringy stories from their life while the song is playing.

title: Turkish Quandale Dingle TikTok Rizz Party
about_text: Turkish Quandale Dingle is a nickname given to a teenage boy; Michael Amato; who appears in a series of TikTok Rizz Party 'Carnival' boys videos dancing at his friend's sweet 16 party. The nickname is a reference to the meme character Quandale Dingle and was given to the boy for a perceivable resemblance between him and an edited photo of rapper YoungBoy NBA with a large nose; which is often used to visually represent Quandale Dingle. The meme is part of a larger series of memes on TikTok Rizz Party lore. The most viral videos of Turkish Quandale were uploaded to TikTok in mid-March 2024 and were popularized in early April through video edits and memes expanding on the fictional lore of Turkish Quandale Dingle. In April 2024; Turkish Quandale revealed himself to be a teen named Mike Amato.

title: Giygas
about_text: Giygas (Japanese: ギーグ Gīgu) is the main antagonist of the video game EarthBound. His appearance in the game is unknown up until the final battle where he is displayed as a red ghoul. The character has become popular among fans due to his dark; creepy appearance and dialogue that is contrary to the light mood of the game; inspiring many works of fan art; video edits and memes referencing him.

title: Simpsonwave
about_text: Simpsonwave is a video remix series featuring clips from the American cartoon sitcom The Simpsons edited with vaporwave-style music and aesthetic visual effects.

title: I Hope You Step on a LEGO

about_text: 'I hope you step on a LEGO' is a phrase often used as a retort in rage comics and reaction images to express one's resentment towards someone who has done something unpleasant as to deserve punishment or ill-fortune; such as accidentally stepping on a LEGO brick.

title: But Your Kids Are Gonna Love It
about_text: But Your Kids Are Gonna Love It is a memorable quote uttered by the character Marty McFly (played by Michael J. Fox) in the 1985 science fiction comedy film Back to the Future. Online; a screen-capture from the scene has been used in image macros and reaction images featuring the quote as a punchline to various jokes about pieces of culture that were not appreciated in their own time.

title: Man Bun Ken
about_text: Man Bun Ken refers to a series of image macros featuring Mattel's millennial rebranding of the Ken doll in which the iconic toy's hair is styled to look like a man bun. Online; people caption pictures of the updated Ken doll with stereotypes about millennial urban dwelling men.

title: Closed On a Sunday Youre My ChickfilA
about_text: Closed On Sunday; You're My Chick-fil-A is a lyric from Kanye West's ninth studio album; Jesus Is King. The lyric was heavily mocked for the metaphor; both because of its awkwardness and the fact that Chick-fil-A; a fast-food restaurant that is closed on Sundays because of founder's Truett Cathy's Christian faith; has been vocally anti-LGBTQ in the past.

title: Shoop Da Whoop
about_text: Shoop da Whoop; also known as I'M A' FIRIN' MAH LAZER!!; is an image macro and catchphrase series based on a frame from the anime series Dragon Ball Z of an edited; blackface depiction of the character Cell shooting a powerful laser stream.

title: Whisper TikTok Character
about_text: Whisper; sometimes known as Whisper the Zombie; is a character that appears on the TikTok page @spookersnz and is depicted as a zombie with gray or white skin; fangs and long black hair who talks in an indecipherable language of growls and clicks. Despite their scary appearance; Whisper is characterized as wholesome and friendly. Whisper first became popularized in late 2022 and became increasingly viral throughout early 2023 as more people began claiming that Whisper is their comfort creature. Whisper notably has a teddy bear named Willow that they call their comfort creature.

title: Miracles  Fucking Magnets How Do They Work
about_text: 'Miracles' is a 2010 rap song by Detroit-based hip hop duo Insane Clown Posse that describes the wonders of the universe and an appreciation for nature’s beauty; while expressing skepticism towards the dogmatic nature of modern science. Upon the YouTube debut of the music video in April 2010; the song instantly went viral and garnered an ironic fandom for its abundance of quotable lyrics; most notably 'fucking magnets; how do they work?'.

title: Nihilist Arbys
about_text: Nilihst Arby's is a novelty Twitter account featuring bleak; pessimistic tweets about the meaninglessness of life and inevitability of death with unenthusiastic recommendations for the fast food restaurant chain Arby's.

title: The Queen Has Breached Containment
about_text: The Queen Has Breached Containment refers to an image macro of an elderly woman climbing the door of the Ministry of Justice in Bulgaria. The woman in the image; which initially surfaced in 2012; became compared to the Queen of England and associated with the phrase 'the Queen has breached containment' in 2019.

title: We Won Mr Stark
about_text: 'We Won; Mr. Stark' is a memorable quote uttered by the character Peter Parker in the film Avengers: Endgame. Online; people have used a screenshot of the Parker speaking the line as a reaction image macro to express feelings of victory.

title: Plane Emoji React
about_text: Plane Emoji React; also known as Plane React; refers to a plane emoji that was temporarily added to Facebook reactions in late July 2018.

title: Why Mafia Isnt an Aesthetic  I Cooka Da Pizza
about_text: I Cooka Da Pizza is a catchphrase used to ridicule Italian accent; culture and Italians in general. The catchphrase was popularized by a reply to a 2021 Twitter thread arguing Why Mafia Isn't a Fucking Aesthetic; with screenshots of the exchange going viral and inspiring memes in the following years.

title: Bench Tails
about_text: Bench Tails is a drawing of Sonic the Hedgehog character Tails from an extremely pornographic fan art webcomic featuring Tails and two small animal children. The first image of the comic showing Tails on a bench has been used as a trolling forum weapon and later in photoshopped parodies as it became notorious.

title: Pokemon Shaming
about_text: Pokemon Shaming; also shortened as Pokeshaming; is a series of illustrations featuring Pokemon characters holding up signs with confessions of their own humorous transgressions that are characteristic of their abilities; drawn in the style of dogshaming photographs

title: Kanye Interrupts  Imma Let You Finish
about_text: Kanye Interrupts; also known as 'Imma let you finish;' refers to an on-air mishap that occurred during Taylor Swift's acceptance speech for Best Female Video at the 2009 MTV Video Music Awards. During the speech; Kanye West suddenly went up to the stage and took the microphone from Swift to declare that Beyonce had 'one of the best music videos of all time.'

title: Redneck Randal
about_text: Redneck Randal is an advice animal image macro series centered around a picture of a mustached young man wearing a sleeveless American pride t-shirt and posing with two thumbs up. The captions typically involve poking fun at the lifestyle of so-called American 'rednecks[2];' a derogatory slang term for people from the southern United States associated with unabashed American pride; beer drinking; and rural life.[3]

title: Let Me Put It In a Language You Can Understand
about_text: Let Me Put It In a Language You Can Understand refers to a memorable scene from 2009 animated film Killer Bean Forever in which a police officer mocks Chinese assassin Jet Bean by speaking in an exaggerated Asian accent. Starting in December 2019; the scene became viral online and later gained popularity in edits on Instagram.

title: Doctor Whooves  Time Turner
about_text: Doctor Whooves[4]; also referred to as 'Doctor Whoof' and 'Time Turner'; is a background earth colt character from My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic. He is a light brown pony with a dark brown mane and light blue eyes. Fans refer to him as the Sole Survivor of Gallopfrey. Some fans thought the colt resembled the tenth incarnation of Doctor Who; David Tennant; due to his spiked brown hair and hourglass cutie mark.

title: Drake and Josh Crash

about_text: Drake and Josh Crash refers to a series of memes based on a memorable scene from Drake & Josh television series in which they drive a car into Oprah Winfrey. Online; the scene has been used in edits on YouTube and Instagram; with the meme seeing a surge in popularity in October 2019.

title: Cheeki Breeki
about_text: 'Cheeki Breeki' is a catchphrase associated with a quote originating from the first-person shooter survival horror game series S.T.A.L.K.E.R.. Since the release of the game in 2007; the stock line often said by non-playable characters of the Bandit factions has become a subject of online parodies among the fans of the game.

title: Spooky Scary Skeletons
about_text: 'Spooky Scary Skeletons' is the name of a children's Halloween song written and preformed by Andrew Gold. Online; the song is primarily associated with the skeleton culture and often used as background music in skeleton-themed memes like 2spooky and Mr. Bones' Wild Ride.

title: JCB Ki Khudai
about_text: JCB Ki Khudai refers to a series of jokes and memes about a brand of industrial excavators found in India. Many of these jokes refer to people's desire to see these machines in action; despite other; conflicting responsibilities.

title: 420 Blaze It
about_text: '420 Blaze it' is a catchphrase used in celebration of smoking marijuana. The phrase is often used ironically to mock cannabis enthusiasts who identify themselves with the stoner subculture.

title: Now This Looks Like a Job For Me
cues_matched: used to/as/when/in; refer(s|ring)? to; reaction (image/gif/video) or 'a reaction'; in which
about_text: Now This Looks Like a Job For Me refers to an image from the music video for the song 'Without Me' by Eminem that began being used as a reaction image in late June of 2017 for situations in which the poster volunteers for a task. The jokes tend to be edgy in content and are popular on meme-focused subreddits.

title: Everybodys So Creative
about_text: Everybody's So Creative! refers to a catchphrase from TikToker @tanaradoublechocolate (also known as Tanara) who went viral for her greenscreen duets with perceivably bad food recipe videos in 2022 and 2023. The cooking videos showed food that was perceivably strange or unhealthy. TikToker @tanaradoublechocolate would narrate the video and then satirically label the food as 'creative.' Her catchphrase became a notable TikTok meme in 2023 as users became familiar with her video style and anticipated something disgusting anytime they heard her voice on their 'For You' page.

title: Simon Cowells Face
about_text: Simon Cowell's Face refers to an image of America's Got Talent judge Simon Cowell on the red carpet for the season 14 live show. The image went viral in mid-2019; due to a Twitter user pointing out an odd difference in Cowell's teeth and eyes leading to wide-spread commentary and conspiracy surrounding Cowell's 'new' face. A derivative using another picture of his insincere smile is also occasionally used as a meme.

title: GIFs With Sound
about_text: GIFs with Sound refer to various pairings of animated GIFs and audio clips that are complementary or perfectly synchronized when played simultaneously.

title: Wake Up And Choose Violence
about_text: Wake Up and Choose Violence is a catchphrase used online to describe a person or animal who is particularly mean-spirited or chaotic; similar to I Think I Will Cause Problems On Purpose. It has been particularly used to comment upon social media threads where one user heavily roasts another; seemingly without provocation.

title: Drip Car
about_text: Drip Car; also known as WhatsApp Car; refers to a photoshopped image of a BMW Z4 car used as an exploitable meme format. In memes; the BMW Z4 typically has its colors changed as well as various logos added to it referencing pop culture or other themes. The image is then set to various music; most commonly trap music; to create the remix video. While originally used with other mobile app logos and notification sounds; such as WhatsApp; it has expanded to be used with any logo and sound combination.

title: Gamer Joker  Gamers Rise Up  We Live in a Society
about_text: Gamer Joker; also known as Gamers Rise Up and We Live in a Society; is an image macro series featuring depictions of the DC Comics supervillain Joker accompanied by intentionally edgy or cringeworthy captions. The memes typically praise video game enthusiasts and criticize women named Veronica who friendzone them in favor of dating 'jocks'. Examples of the meme are frequently submitted to the Gang Weed Facebook page.[9]

title: Soraya Montenegro
about_text: Soraya Montenegro is a fictional character and prominent antagonist featured in the popular Mexican telenovela María la del Barrio. On the Internet; the character has gained an ironic fan following for her traits of extreme jealousy and frequent resort to violence; including murder.

title: Its Wizard Time Fireball
about_text: It's Wizard Time; Fireball refers to a series of viral exploitable audio clips from the Doom mod Reelism 2 spoken by the wizard enemies; most notably the lines; 'It's wizard time; motherfucker' and 'Fireball!' The first version of the Reelism 2 mod was released in April 2022 and the voice clips were uploaded as a compilation to YouTube that August; inspiring memes including animations and lip dubs on YouTube and TikTok over the following months using a cut of the wizard saying; 'It's wizard time motherfuckers; fireball!'

title: Kanye West Heil Hitler Song
about_text: The Kanye West 'Heil Hitler' Song refers to an unreleased track by rapper Kanye West (Ye) in which the chorus repeats; 'Nigga; Heil Hitler.' It was initially previewed on an April 2025 livestream hosted by Kick streamer Sneako. The clip of Ye; Sneako and others listening to the track went viral on Twitter / X in late April and early May 2025; as many had negative reactions to it; adding to Kanye West's antisemitism controversies during the 2020s. Memes and viral tweets compared the track to Ye's other song 'COUSINS' from his upcoming album (purportedly called CUCK) with Dave Blunts.

title: Homelander
about_text: Homelander is a main character and antagonist in the comic and Amazon TV series The Boys; created by Garth Ennis. Homelander is seen as a dark version of Superman in that he has similar powers and abilities; but is noticeably unhinged and evil. In both the comics and show; various scenes and expressions associated with Homelander have become memes; such as the Stressed Homelander or Homelander Gets Applause formats depicting actor Antony Starr.

title: The Flying Spaghetti Monster
about_text: The Flying Spaghetti Monster is a satirical deity for the Church of the Flying Spaghetti Monster; a parody religious organization with followers known as 'Pastafarians.' The being resembles a large floating mass of cylindrical pasta with two large eyestalks that carries meatballs on each side of its body.

title: Facebook Name Change To Meta
about_text: Facebook Name Change To Meta Announcement refer to memes that use images from the name change announcement to create memes; either by changing stills from the keynote itself or taking images from it and photoshopping them into other images to create memes. These types of memes are one of many that have spawned from the singular keynote and concept of the Facebook name change.

title: Send Me 5 and See What Happens
about_text: Send Me $5 and See What Happens is a prank on Tinder in which women request that matches send them $5; and then immediately un-match following receipt of the money. While initially started by college student Maggie Archer; the practice was subsequently adopted by other Tinder users.

title: Kendall Jenner Cucumber Girl

The clip was largely mocked on social media where it appeared in reaction videos and later earned her the nickname 'Cucumber Girl' from her sister; Kylie.

title: Patrick Bateman with An Axe

In memes; the still of the scene is typically used when another person says or does something that the other person hates or doesn't agree with. It's also used in Rage Comics as a reaction to proceeding captions; as well as being used as an exploitable where it is photoshopped to include elements from other pop culture references.

title: hhgregg Christmas in July
about_text: hhgregg Christmas in July refers to a 2010 Christmas in July television commercial from the American specialty retailer hhgregg. The advertisement has been used as a source in YouTube Poop Music Videos (YTPMVs) as well as other edits.

title: Irish Slavery Myth
about_text: The Irish Slavery Myth refers to controversial claims made by historians who assert that Irish immigrants were held as chattel slaves upon their arrival in the North American colonies; with some believing that enslavement of the Irish settlers preceded that of the African and Native Americans during the 16th century. Since its online emergence in the mid-2000s; the theory has been largely refuted by those who emphasize the distinction between indentured servitude of involuntary nature and slavery.

title: Fish Chicken Smoking a Cigarette
about_text: Fish Chicken Smoking a Cigarette refers to an image of a trout's head poking out of the torso of a chicken with a cigarette in its mouth. Due to its high potency of the WTF factor; the image has been widely passed around Internet humor sites.

title: Toxic Masculinity
about_text: Toxic Masculinity is a neologism used to describe normative masculine behavior in Western society associated with behaviors linked to dominance; misogyny; homophobia and violence.

title: Dolphin Pussy Jelly
about_text: Dolphin Pussy Jelly refers to a viral 2020 post to Facebook in which a user falsely claims that the vaginal secretion of dolphins; which the user refers to as 'dolphin pussy jelly;' makes male dolphins ejaculate repeatedly. They go on to describe how scientists once tested the secretion on a monkey's penis and it died of a heart attack due to too much sex and advocate for releasing the jelly as a form of suicide. The post inspired a number of photoshops and image macros; and later became a copypasta.

title: Oh Fuck Yeah Spread It

about_text: Oh Fuck Yeah Spread It refers to a screenshot of pornographic actress Riley Reid saying 'Oh Fuck Yeah Spread It' during a scene. It is used out of context as a reaction image describing something being spread.

title: Ned Leeds
about_text: Ned Leeds is a fictional Marvel Comics character from the Spider-Man franchise who is portrayed in the Marvel Cinematic Universe by Jacob Batalon. Though he was an obscure character to casual audiences prior to his film debut in 2017's Spider-Man: Homecoming; Batalon's portrayal of the character has become a fan favorite for his awkward personality and humorous dialogue and featured in numerous memetic content edits infused with varying levels of irony.

title: Conflicted Steve Harvey

about_text: Conflicted Steve Harvey refers to a popular reaction image featuring two images of television host Steve Harvey super-imposed onto each other. One image shows Harvey laughing; and the other of him looking worried or distressed. The image is paired with captions in which one may laugh but simultaneously feels anxiety.

title: Sneezing Baby Panda
about_text: Sneezing Baby Panda is a viral video uploaded to YouTube on November 6th; 2006 that depicts a mother panda at the Wolong Panda Breeding Centre munching on bamboo while her baby cub sleeps at her feet. Out of nowhere; the baby sneezes; startling the mother for a second before she returns to her food. As of August 2013; the video was been viewed more than 162.7 million times and was named #10 on Urlesque's[1] 100 Most Iconic Internet Videos in April 2009. [[more information needed… just who is this mysterious Panda?!]]

title: Bills Mafia
about_text: #BillsMafia is a hashtag used to encapsulate the fandom of the National Football League's Buffalo Bills. The hashtag gained infamy when it became synonymous with Bills fans who performed amateur stunts; such as crashing through tables; during tailgate parties for Bills games.

title: I Need You To Distract Kang
about_text: I Need You To Distract Kang is a copypasta and catchphrase that was popularized in early 2023. Several X users shared fake edited 'leaks' from the upcoming MCU film Avengers: Secret Wars; with one image showing Tony Stark (Iron Man) asking Wolverine (Logan Howlett) to 'distract Kang;' saying; 'Logan; I need you to distract Kang.' The phrase came to be used to mock subsequent fake leaks; with some internet users photoshopping different characters into fake Avengers leaks.

title: Raymond
about_text: Raymond is a character from the Animal Crossing series of life-simulation games published by Nintendo. After being revealed that he would be included in Animal Crossing: New Horizons the character started gaining popularity online; to the point people started to sell him so other people could add him to their islands.

title: You Cannot Kill Me In A Way That Matters
about_text: You Cannot Kill Me In A Way That Matters is a phrase from a 2018 Tumblr shitpost story about mushrooms. It has since become part of Tumblr culture and spawned fan art; dramatic readings; merchandise and music.

title: X Y is X Redundant Adjectives are Redundant
about_text: (X) (Y) is (X) ('redundant adjectives are redundant') is a formulaic sentence structure typically used in online conversations for redundancy and emphasis. Some of the more popular examples include 'Longcat is looooong'; 'Successful troll is successful' and 'old pic is old.'

title: This Dog Does 11640 Damage per Second
about_text: This Dog Does 11;640 Damage per Second refers to a series of memes in which various animals are captioned with the phrasal template 'This X Does 11;640 Damage per Second.' Spawned by a viral Elden Ring video explaining a bug that results the enemy NPC Rotten Stray dealing huge damage per second; the caption was popularized on social media in late March 2022.

title: Youre In The Club And This Guy Slaps Your Girlfriends Ass
about_text: 'You're In The Club And This Guy Slaps Your Girlfriend's Ass' is a catchphrase associated to a picture of a muscular black man; which has been often parodied by being used with images of people or objects with ridicule or bizarre looks.

title: Fashtag
about_text: Fashtag is a hoax orchestrated by members of 4chan's and 8chan's /pol/ boards attempting to trick people into thinking the 'hashtag': is an alt-right hate symbol as part of a campaign known as 'Operation Bash the Hash.'

title: Im Fuckin Ballin
